info.title: GenomeHubs API
info.description:
GenomeHubs Application Programming Interface.

Provides a low-level API to retrieve records, look up taxa, search and generate reports across all taxon, assembly, and sample data.

See the [GenomeHubs documentation](https://genomehubs.org) for further details.

get.summary: Count using URL query string parameters
get.description:
Convenience endpoint that accepts the same URL parameters as the GoaT UI.

Returns the total matching record count without fetching results.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: GoaT UI URL or query string (e.g. `?result=taxon&query=tax_tree(Mammalia)%20AND%20genome_size`)

post.summary: Count records matching a search query
post.description:
Fetch the record count for a search query without including any of the results.

`/count` supports all relevant `/search` parameters to allow a record count to be obtained for a query prior to a full search.

value.query_yaml:
index: assembly
taxa:
  - Mammalia
taxon_filter_type: tree

Mammalia species count.summary: Count species in Mammalia with a genome size estimate

value.query_yaml:
index: taxon
taxa:
  - Mammalia
taxon_filter_type: tree
fields:
  - name: genome_size

post.summary: Count records for multiple queries in a single request
post.description: Execute up to 100 count queries simultaneously using a single POST. Reduces network round-trips for parallel counts.

Two counts.summary: Count Mammalia and Insecta taxa in parallel

get.summary: Find record IDs matching a search term
get.description: Find record IDs matching a search term based on full or partial matches to a record ID, name, synonym or other identifier.

post.summary: Resolve up to 100 search terms to record IDs in one request
post.description: Runs each item through the same three-stage waterfall (SAYT → wildcard → suggest) as `GET /lookup`, using at most 3 `_msearch` calls for the entire batch. Results are returned in input order.

get.description: Returns available indices, taxonomies, taxonomic ranks, and API versions in a single response.

200.description: Aggregated metadata: indices, taxonomies, ranks, and versions

get.summary: Returns available fields for a given result type
get.description: Returns field definitions, types, and synonyms for the specified result index (taxon, assembly, or sample).
Returns the names of all searchable indices available in this hub (e.g. taxon, assembly, sample).
Returns all supported taxonomic ranks for this hub (e.g. species, genus, family).
Returns the names of all taxonomies available in this hub (e.g. ncbi).

get.summary: Get PhyloPic silhouette for a taxon
get.description: Fetches a PhyloPic silhouette image record for a given NCBI taxon ID. Results are cached per API build cycle.

post.summary: Resolve PhyloPic silhouettes for up to 200 taxa
post.description: Fetches PhyloPic silhouette image records for up to 200 NCBI taxon IDs in a single POST. Results are returned as a map keyed by taxon ID.

post.summary: Generate a positional report (oxford / ribbon / painting)
post.description:
Returns positional comparison data for one or more assemblies. Supports oxford (2-assembly dot-plot), ribbon (N-assembly synteny), and painting (single-assembly chromosome map).

`query_yaml` is **optional**. When omitted, features are filtered by the `group_by` value (used as `primary_type`) automatically.

Set `index: feature` in `query_yaml` to apply attribute filters directly to the feature index (e.g. `sequence_length >= 1000000`).
Set `index: taxon` + `taxa:` to restrict features to a given taxon subtree.

Oxford — feature attribute filter.summary: Oxford dot-plot restricted to chromosomes ≥ 10 Mb
Oxford — feature attribute filter.description: When `index: feature` is set, attribute filters first resolve which sequences (chromosomes/scaffolds) match, then only features on those sequences are returned. This selects busco genes located on sequences with `length >= 10000000` (10 Mb).

value.positional_yaml:
report: oxford
group_by: metazoa_odb10-busco-gene
assemblies:
  - GCA_905147045.1
  - GCA_902806685.2
value.query_yaml:
index: feature
attributes:
  - name: length
    operator: ge
    value: "10000000"

Oxford — no query filter.summary: Oxford dot-plot, features filtered by group_by only

value.positional_yaml:
report: oxford
group_by: busco_gene
assemblies:
  - GCA_905147045.1
  - GCA_902806685.2
window_size: 1000000

Oxford — taxon filter.summary: Oxford dot-plot with taxon subtree filter

value.positional_yaml:
report: oxford
group_by: busco_gene
assemblies:
  - GCA_905147045.1
  - GCA_902806685.2
window_size: 1000000
value.query_yaml:
index: taxon
taxa:
  - Lepidoptera
taxon_filter_type: tree

Painting.summary: Chromosome painting coloured by BUSCO status

value.positional_yaml:
report: painting
group_by: busco_gene
assemblies:
  - GCA_905147045.1
cat: busco_status
window_size: 500000

get.description: Returns all field values for one or more specific records identified by their record ID.

post.summary: Fetch up to 1,000 records by ID in one request
post.description: Accepts an explicit array of record IDs and returns all found records in a single `_mget` call. Prefer this over repeated `GET /record` calls.
Returns aggregated data for reports including histograms, scatter plots, trees, and other visualisations.

get.summary: Search using URL query string parameters
get.description:
Convenience endpoint that accepts the same URL parameters as the GoaT UI.

Paste a GoaT browser URL into the `url` parameter to reproduce the same query via the API.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: GoaT UI URL or query string (e.g. `?result=taxon&query=tax_tree(Mammalia)%20AND%20genome_size&fields=genome_size&size=10`)

post.summary: Fetch records matching a search query
post.description: Returns records matching a query. Supports all major query parameters including filtering, sorting, and pagination.

Large mammal assemblies.summary: Search assemblies for mammals with genome >= 1 Gbp

value.query_yaml:
index: assembly
taxa:
  - Mammalia
taxon_filter_type: tree
fields:
  - name: assembly_span
    modifiers: [min=1000000000]

Mammalia with genome size.summary: Search for Mammalia taxa with a genome size estimate

value.params_yaml:
size: 10
fields: genome_size
include_estimates: true
taxonomy: ncbi
value.query_yaml:
index: taxon
taxa:
  - Mammalia
taxon_filter_type: tree
fields:
  - name: genome_size

post.summary: Fetch records for multiple search queries in a single request
post.description: Execute up to 100 search queries simultaneously using a single POST. Reduces network round-trips for parallel searches.

Two searches.summary: Search Mammalia and Insecta in parallel

get.description: Returns health status of the API and lists all supported endpoint paths.

get.summary: Fetch aggregated summary statistics for a record
get.description: Returns aggregated summary statistics (min, max, mean, etc.) for specific fields of a given record.

post.summary: Fetch aggregated summary statistics for multiple records in parallel
post.description: Runs multiple `/summary` queries in a single request.  Each item in `queries` is processed independently and results are returned in the same order.

Two records.summary: Fetch genome_size summary for two taxa

ApiStatus.description:
Uniform status block present in every v3 API response.

Metadata-only endpoints (taxonomies, ranks, indices) omit `hits` and `took`.
Query endpoints always populate all four fields.

searches.description: Array of queries to count in batch (max 100).

total.description: Sum of all individual result counts.

unique.description: Count of unique results across all searches (only set if all searches use same index).

FeatureIndexVersion.description:
Whether the feature index uses the v1 nested-attributes-only structure
or the v2 flat-field structure expected by `/api/v3/positional`.

results.description: Per-item lookup results, in input order.

MetadataResponse.description:
Aggregated metadata response combining indices, taxonomies, ranks, and versions in a
single round-trip. Fields are excluded because they require a `?result=`
qualifier and cannot be returned without a parameter.

feature_types.description: Distinct `feature_type` attribute values from the feature index.

versions.description:
Known data release versions. Currently a single-element list; multi-version
support will extend this when the API serves more than one release.

PhylopicRecord.description: A silhouette image resolved from PhyloPic for one taxon.

attribution.description: Free-text attribution string from the contributor.

build.description: PhyloPic build number at time of fetch.

image_name.description: Scientific name of the taxon the image directly illustrates.

image_rank.description: Taxonomic rank of the node the image represents.

license.description: SPDX licence identifier (e.g. `"CC0-1.0"`) derived from the licence URL.

raster_url.description: URL of a raster (PNG) file — second-largest, or largest if only one exists.

ratio.description: Aspect ratio (width / height) of the raster image.

source_url.description: Canonical URL of this image on phylopic.org.

taxon_id.description: NCBI taxon ID the record was requested for.

vector_url.description: URL of the SVG vector file (resolution-independent). Prefer over `raster_url` in UI.

PhylopicSource.description: How the image was resolved relative to the requested taxon.

display.description: Optional display spec (JSON) applied to the returned PlotSpec.

include_plot_spec.description: When true, produce a serialisable `PlotSpec` alongside the report.

query_yaml.description:
Optional query filter.  When omitted features are filtered only by
`group_by` (as `primary_type`) and the `assemblies` list.

record_ids.description:
Record IDs to fetch (max 1,000).

IDs may be fully-qualified (e.g. `"taxon-9606"`) or bare numeric
strings; bare IDs are auto-prefixed with `"<result>-"`.

searches.description: Array of queries to search in batch (max 100).

error.description: Error message for this query if it failed independently.

lineage_summary.description: Per-rank ancestor aggregation results (only present when `lineage_rank_summary` was requested).

lineage_summary_background.description:
Background per-rank ancestor aggregation results: distributions
computed across all descendant taxa for the ancestor (default).

results.description: Document results in the same `{index, id, score, result}` format as `/api/v3/search`.

search_after.description: Cursor for the next page, if more results exist.

total.description: Total number of matching documents in ES (may exceed `results` length).

lineage_summary.description:
Per-rank ancestor aggregation results.

Shape: `{rank: {ancestor_taxon_id: {field: distribution}}}`.
Only present when `lineage_rank_summary` was requested.

lineage_summary_background.description:
Background per-rank ancestor aggregation results: distributions
computed across all descendant taxa for the ancestor (default).

search_after.description: Cursor for the next page, if more results exist.

url.description: URL that was built for this query (for debugging/reproduction).

queries.description: Array of summary requests to run in parallel (max 100).

tags.name: Data
tags.description: Retrieve search results, records and reports

tags.name: Metadata
tags.description: List available indices, fields, taxonomies and ranks
